phases.name: foundation
phases.label: Foundation Phase
phases.focus: Basic movement, ball familiarisation, footwork fundamentals
phases.description: Players at this stage are developing fundamental movement skills and learning the basics of netball. Emphasis is on enjoyment, inclusion, and correct footwork before any tactical concepts are introduced.
physical_milestones: Can land on two feet correctly after receiving
Can pivot without stepping
Can perform a chest pass with correct hand position
Can dodge and lead to receive
Comfortable running and changing direction
technical_milestones: Knows the footwork rule and applies it consistently
Can throw a chest pass to a stationary partner
Understands that you cannot run with the ball
Can identify the goal circle
Knows basic court boundaries

phases.name: development
phases.label: Development Phase
phases.focus: Position roles, 0.9m rule, basic attacking combinations
phases.description: Players begin learning their specific positions and the rules governing those zones. Attacking and defensive combinations are introduced. Centre pass patterns are drilled repeatedly as the most important restart in the game.
physical_milestones: Consistent 0.9m discipline under match pressure
Can maintain defensive stance for a full quarter
Explosive first step from standing position
Can change direction at speed without losing balance
Adequate aerobic base for 4 x 10-minute quarters
technical_milestones: Understands their position zone and off-side rule
Can execute a centre pass pattern in role
Delivers the ball accurately within 3 seconds
Can intercept a telegraphed pass
GS/GA can shoot from inside the goal circle under light pressure

phases.name: performance
phases.label: Performance Phase
phases.focus: Tactical awareness, intercept patterns, shooting under load
phases.description: Players at this level are competing at district and provincial level. Training shifts to game-based scenarios. Defenders focus on reading the ball; shooters focus on composure under contested defence. Centre court players drill timing and width.

session_structure.component: Full Game or Intense Conditioned Game

physical_milestones: Can sustain high-intensity play for a full game
Reaction time under 0.3s on intercept attempts
Shooters maintain 70%+ accuracy under contested defence
Full vertical leap reach for goal-circle contests
No footwork infringements under match pressure
technical_milestones: Reads centre pass options and selects first receiver correctly
Can execute 3 or more set attacking combinations
Defenders can apply 0.9m pressure and time movement
GS can hold position against physical GK/GD
Centre distributes evenly to both wings and back

phases.name: elite
phases.label: Elite Pathway
phases.focus: Provincial and national selection — consistent high performance
phases.description: Players in the elite pathway are being monitored by provincial and national selectors. Training includes video analysis, individual AI feedback on stats, and position-specific high-intensity conditioning. ZINA pathway targets are tracked per session.
physical_milestones: Elite vertical reach — above national average for position
Sub-5.5s 40m sprint from standing start
Can play at full intensity for 4 x 15-minute quarters
No significant injury in the past 12 months
Strength benchmarks met for their position group
technical_milestones: Consistent selection in provincial training squad
AI performance score above 75/100 on Grassroots platform
Can lead a centre pass combination from any position
Match stats logged and trending upward over 3-month block
Shortlisted on ZINA national talent database
